session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8513 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the PR feedback classifier skill and run it with the specified PR number.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the required command to fetch PR feedback:

[View workflow run](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22532232001)", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8513#issuecomment-3978699057" }, { "id": 3978723660, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 12:24:15 PT

Found 4 violations across 3 files. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim code blocks (new additions) — None found in new diff lines
❌ Inaccurate source pointers — Found in docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:83 and docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md:34
❌ Inaccurate prose claims — Found in docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:79
❌ Drift risk (line numbers in prose) — Found in docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md:130
⚠️ Pre-existing verbatim code block (not in diff, cannot inline-comment) — docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:66-71 (fire-and-forget try/except copies land_learn.py:188-191)

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:79`: Inaccurate — `_extract_learn_plan_number()` claimed to be at `app.py:64-73` but is in `src/erk/tui/operations/logic.py:17`
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:83`: Inaccurate source pointer — wrong file (`app.py`), invalid line range (882-920, file only has 532 lines)
- `docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md:34`: Inaccurate source pointer — `action_one_shot_prompt` is in `src/erk/tui/actions/palette.py:250-264`, not `app.py:476-510`
- `docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md:130`: Drift risk — specific line number reference (`--summary at line 501-505`) will shift as code evolves

### Files Reviewed
| File | Verdict | Issues |
|------|---------|--------|
| `docs/learned/architecture/index.md` | KEEP | 0 (index-only change) |
| `docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md` | SIMPLIFY | 1 drift risk (line number in prose) |
| `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md` | SIMPLIFY | 2 inaccurate claims + 1 pre-existing verbatim block |
| `docs/learned/planning/index.md` | KEEP | 0 (index-only change) |
| `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md` | KEEP | 0 |
| `docs/learned/tui/index.md` | KEEP | 0 (index-only change) |
| `docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md` | SIMPLIFY | 1 inaccurate source pointer |
| `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md` | KEEP | 0 |

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 12:24:15 PT**: Found 4 violations across 3 files: 2 inaccurate source pointers (wrong file for app integration code), 1 inaccurate prose claim (_extract_learn_plan_number wrong file), 1 drift risk (line numbers in inference-hoisting). Pre-existing verbatim fire-and-forget block noted in summary.

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8513#issuecomment-3978723660" }, { "id": 3980819868, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was updated by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 8513
```

### What is this?

[Erk](https://github.com/schrockn/erk) is a CLI tool for plan-oriented agentic
engineering. The `pr-address` workflow uses Claude to read PR review comments
and address them automatically.

### Changes Made

The changes replace verbatim code block snippets with source pointer comments and more concise prose descriptions. Instead of showing full function signatures and dataclass definitions inline, the documentation now references the actual source locations (e.g., `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py`) and describes functionality in narrative form. This reduces documentation noise while maintaining traceability to source code, and simplifies descriptions by focusing on semantic purpose rather than exact implementation details.

### Details

- **Model:** claude-opus-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22550546790)
- **Commits added:** 1

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8513#issuecomment-3980819868"

session.assistant:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me analyze this data and produce the classified JSON output according to the skill's requirements:
Based on the PR feedback data collected:
1. **PR Info**: PR 8513 is "Consolidate documentation from Feb 28 learn sessions (batch 2)" 2. **Review Threads**: None exist (empty array) 3. **Discussion Comments**: All 4 are informational (status updates and audit reports from automated processes) 4. **File Restructuring**: Documentation files only (no renames/moves detected - just additions)
According to the classification model: - The first comment is a plan submission status (informational) - The second comment is a workflow status update (informational) - The third comment is an audit report from `github-actions[bot]` reporting documentation violations (informational/reference) - The fourth comment is a PR address workflow update (informational)
All comments are discussion comments that are purely informational (CI status updates, Graphite stack comments, automated PR operations) and do not require action. There are no review threads at all.
